title: [Epic] Technical Debt Audit Feedback & Microservice Hardening
body: This epic tracks follow-up hardening items, caching optimizations, and resiliency improvements arising from the initial Technical Debt Audit remediations.

issues.title: [Security] Implement JWKS Cache Expiration and Key Rotation Support
issues.body:
**Description:**
The current implementation of `fetchLiferayJwks` in `loggingMiddleware.cjs` caches Liferay's JWKS key set indefinitely in memory. 

**Technical Impact:**
If Liferay rotates its signing keys, the microservice will continue validating against the stale cached JWKS, causing all subsequent API requests to fail with `Invalid JWT` errors until manual restart.

**Proposed Remediation:**
Introduce a Cache TTL (e.g. 24 hours) for the JWKS or check if the token's `kid` is missing from the cache, forcing a refresh of the key set.

(Follow-up to #271)

issues.title: [Refactor] Implement Connection Probe Caching in HealthService
issues.body:
**Description:**
`HealthService.checkLiferay()` executes a live Liferay connection check (`liferay.rest.testConnection`) on every execution of the health check endpoint.

**Technical Impact:**
High polling frequency by external orchestrators/load-balancers will flood Liferay with connection test requests, causing unnecessary server load and API rate-limiting risks.

**Proposed Remediation:**
Cache the Liferay connection health status for 15-30 seconds to bypass live network calls on rapid consecutive probes.

(Follow-up to #276)

issues.title: [Refactor] Add Re-entrancy Guard to Server Graceful Shutdown
issues.body:
**Description:**
`gracefulShutdown()` in `server.cjs` is invoked on uncaughtExceptions and unhandledRejections but does not check if a shutdown is already in progress.

**Technical Impact:**
Exceptions thrown during the shutdown process itself will trigger recursive calls to `gracefulShutdown`, causing infinite crash loops and leaving SQLite files locked/corrupted.

**Proposed Remediation:**
Add a boolean guard flag (`isShuttingDown`) to return immediately if shutdown is already active.

(Follow-up to #277)

issues.title: [Refactor] Replace Static Concurrency Chunking with Sliding-Window Queue
issues.body:
**Description:**
`deleteProducts.cjs` processes parallel deletions by dividing the array into static chunks (e.g., of size 5) and waiting for all items in the chunk to finish before launching the next batch.

**Technical Impact:**
Head-of-line blocking. If a single item in a chunk takes 3 seconds and others take 0.1 seconds, the queue is blocked from processing new items, reducing throughput.

**Proposed Remediation:**
Use a sliding-window concurrency queue (e.g., using `p-limit` or a custom sliding queue) that launches a new deletion task immediately as soon as any active task completes.

(Follow-up to #273)
